result.stdout:
Description:
  Package published .NET applications into Linux, Windows, and macOS-friendly installers.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose  Enable verbose logging
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information
  --version                   Show version information

Commands:
  deb, debian, pack-deb    Create a Debian (.deb) installer for Debian and Ubuntu based distributions.
  pack-rpm, rpm            Create an RPM (.rpm) package suitable for Fedora, openSUSE, and other RPM-based distributions.
  appimage, pack-appimage  Create a portable AppImage (.AppImage) from a published directory. Use subcommands for AppDir workflows.
  dmg, pack-dmg            Create a macOS disk image (.dmg) with a native HFS+ payload wrapped in UDIF (DMG) format.
  msix                     MSIX packaging (experimental)
  exe                      Windows self-extracting installer (.exe). If --stub is not provided, the tool downloads the appropriate stub from GitHub Releases.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Package published .NET applications into Linux, Windows, and macOS-friendly installers.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose  Enable verbose logging
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information
  --version                   Show version information

Commands:
  deb, debian, pack-deb    Create a Debian (.deb) installer for Debian and Ubuntu based distributions.
  pack-rpm, rpm            Create an RPM (.rpm) package suitable for Fedora, openSUSE, and other RPM-based distributions.
  appimage, pack-appimage  Create a portable AppImage (.AppImage) from a published directory. Use subcommands for AppDir workflows.
  dmg, pack-dmg            Create a macOS disk image (.dmg) with a native HFS+ payload wrapped in UDIF (DMG) format.
  msix                     MSIX packaging (experimental)
  exe                      Windows self-extracting installer (.exe). If --stub is not provided, the tool downloads the appropriate stub from GitHub Releases.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Windows self-extracting installer (.exe). If --stub is not provided, the tool downloads the appropriate stub from GitHub Releases.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool exe [command] [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                               The input directory (publish output)
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                     Output installer .exe
  --stub <stub>                                                    Path to the prebuilt stub (WinExe) to concatenate (optional if repo layout is present)
  --setup-logo <setup-logo>                                        Path to a logo image displayed by the installer and uninstaller wizards
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>  Application name
  --comment <comment>                                              Comment / long description
  --version <version>                                              Version
  --appId <appId>                                                  Application Id (Reverse DNS typical)
  --company, --vendor <vendor>                                     Vendor/Publisher
  --executable-name <executable-name>                              Name of your application's executable
  --arch <arch>                                                    Target architecture for the stub (x64, arm64)
  --homepage <homepage>                                            Home page of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                              Screenshot URLs
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                       Enable verbose logging

Commands:
  from-project  Publish a .NET project and build a Windows self-extracting installer (.exe). If --stub is not provided, the tool downloads the appropriate stub from GitHub Releases.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Publish a .NET project and build a Windows self-extracting installer (.exe). If --stub is not provided, the tool downloads the appropriate stub from GitHub Releases.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool exe from-project [options]

Options:
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)                                   Path to the .csproj file
  --arch <arch>                                                    Target architecture (x64, arm64)
  --self-contained                                                 Publish self-contained [Deprecated]
  --configuration <configuration>                                  Build configuration [default: Release]
  --single-file                                                    Publish single-file
  --trimmed                                                        Enable trimming
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                     Output installer .exe
  --stub <stub>                                                    Path to the prebuilt stub (WinExe) to concatenate (optional if repo layout is present)
  --setup-logo <setup-logo>                                        Path to a logo image displayed by the installer and uninstaller wizards
  --setup-logo <setup-logo>                                        Path to a logo image displayed by the installer and uninstaller wizards
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>  Application name
  --comment <comment>                                              Comment / long description
  --version <version>                                              Version
  --appId <appId>                                                  Application Id (Reverse DNS typical)
  --company, --vendor <vendor>                                     Vendor/Publisher
  --executable-name <executable-name>                              Name of your application's executable
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                       Enable verbose logging

result.stdout:
Description:
  MSIX packaging (experimental)

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool msix [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose  Enable verbose logging

Commands:
  pack          Create an MSIX from a directory (expects AppxManifest.xml in the tree or pre-baked metadata). Experimental.
  from-project  Publish a .NET project and build an MSIX from the published output (expects manifest/assets).

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a portable AppImage (.AppImage) from a published directory. Use subcommands for AppDir workflows.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool appimage [command] [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            Published application directory (for example: bin/Release/<tfm>/publish)
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Destination path for the generated .AppImage file
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>                                               Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                                                                    Enable verbose logging

Commands:
  appdir        Creates an AppDir from a directory (does not package an .AppImage). For .NET apps, pass the publish directory.
  from-appdir   Creates an AppImage from an existing AppDir directory.
  from-project  Publish a .NET project and build an AppImage from the published output.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates an AppImage from an existing AppDir directory.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool appimage from-appdir [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            The AppDir directory to package
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Output .AppImage file
  --executable-relative-path <executable-relative-path>                                                         Executable inside the AppDir (relative), e.g., usr/bin/MyApp
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>                                               Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords

  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                                                                    Enable verbose logging

  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                                                                    Enable verbose logging

  from-project  Publish a .NET project and build a Debian .deb from the published output.

  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                                                                    Enable verbose logging

Commands:
  from-project  Publish a .NET project and build a Debian .deb from the published output.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a macOS disk image (.dmg) with a native HFS+ payload wrapped in UDIF (DMG) format.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool dmg [command] [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            Published application directory (for example: bin/Release/<tfm>/publish)
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Destination path for the generated .dmg file
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>                                               Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  --with-default-layout                                                                                         Add a default Finder layout (background image, Applications link positioning) when none is provided
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                                                                    Enable verbose logging

Commands:
  from-project  Publish a .NET project and build a .dmg from the published output (.app bundle auto-generated if missing). Experimental.
  verify        Verify that a .dmg file has a macOS-friendly structure (ISO/UDTO or UDIF).

result.stdout:
Description:
  Publish a .NET project and build a .dmg from the published output (.app bundle auto-generated if missing). Experimental.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool dmg from-project [options]

Options:
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)                                   Path to the .csproj file
  --arch <arch>                                                    Target architecture (x64, arm64)
  --self-contained                                                 Publish self-contained [Deprecated]
  --configuration <configuration>                                  Build configuration [default: Release]
  --single-file                                                    Publish single-file
  --trimmed                                                        Enable trimming
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                     Output .dmg file
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>  Application name / volume name
  --compress                                                       Compress the DMG payload (bzip2/UDZO-like)
  --with-default-layout                                            Add a default Finder layout (background image, Applications link positioning) when none is provided
  --homepage <homepage>                                            Home page of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                              Screenshot URLs
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                       Enable verbose logging

commands.payload:
Description:
  Publish a .NET project and build a .dmg from the published output (.app bundle auto-generated if missing). Experimental.

Usage:

  --project <project> (REQUIRED)                                   Path to the .csproj file
  --arch <arch>                                                    Target architecture (x64, arm64)
  --self-contained                                                 Publish self-contained [Deprecated]
  --configuration <configuration>                                  Build configuration [default: Release]
  --single-file                                                    Publish single-file
  --trimmed                                                        Enable trimming
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                     Output .dmg file
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>  Application name / volume name
  --compress                                                       Compress the DMG payload (bzip2/UDZO-like)
  --with-default-layout                                            Add a default Finder layout (background image, Applications link positioning) when none is provided
  --homepage <homepage>                                            Home page of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                              Screenshot URLs
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                       Enable verbose logging

Options:
  --file <file> (REQUIRED)    Path to the .dmg file
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose  Enable verbose logging

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create an RPM (.rpm) package suitable for Fedora, openSUSE, and other RPM-based distributions.

Usage:
  DotnetPackaging.Tool rpm [command] [options]

Options:
  --directory <directory> (REQUIRED)                                                                            Published application directory (for example: bin/Release/<tfm>/publish)
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                                                                                  Destination path for the generated .rpm file
  --application-name, --appName, --productName <application-name>                                               Application name
  --wm-class <wm-class>                                                                                         Startup WM Class
  --main-category <Audio|AudioVideo|Development|Education|Game|Graphics|Network|Office|Settings|Utility|Video>  Main category
  --keywords <keywords>                                                                                         Keywords
  --comment <comment>                                                                                           Comment
  --icon <icon>                                                                                                 Path to the application icon
  --additional-categories <additional-categories>                                                               Additional categories
  --version <version>                                                                                           Version
  --homepage <homepage>                                                                                         Home page of the application
  --license <license>                                                                                           License of the application
  --screenshot-urls <screenshot-urls>                                                                           Screenshot URLs
  --summary <summary>                                                                                           Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.
  --appId <appId>                                                                                               Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication
  --executable-name <executable-name>                                                                           Name of your application's executable
  --is-terminal                                                                                                 Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                Show help and usage information
  -d, -v, --debug, --verbose                                                                                    Enable verbose logging

Commands:
  from-project  Publish a .NET project and build an RPM from the published output (no code duplication; library drives the pipeline).

commands.payload:
